mnemonics: BEND (Binomial, Exponential, Normal, Discrete) - The key distributions will BEND to your will.
P.D. (Probability Distributions) are like a Doctor's Prescription: They tell you the exact chance (dose) of an outcome (symptom) occurring.
keyPoints: A probability distribution describes how probabilities are distributed over the values of a random variable.
The sum of all probabilities in a discrete distribution must equal 1.
The area under the curve of a continuous probability distribution function (PDF) must equal 1.
commonMistakes: Confusing a Probability Mass Function (PMF) for discrete variables with a Probability Density Function (PDF) for continuous variables.
Forgetting that for a continuous distribution, the probability of a single, exact point (e.g., P(X=2)) is zero; probability is measured over an interval.
